zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan de Grey Baroness Willoughby於1306年出生在英國奧可福爾洛瑟費爾德 格斯時，她的父親Sir John de Grey 1st Baron Grey of Rotherfield29歲，母親Margaret Oddingseles29歲。她和Sir John Leeke IV至少有2個兒子。她去世於1385年4月在英國素瑞可洛頓享年79歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan de Grey Baroness Willoughby于1306年出生在英国奧可福爾洛瑟費爾德 格斯时，她的父亲Sir John de Grey 1st Baron Grey of Rotherfield29岁，母亲Margaret Oddingseles29岁。她和Sir John Leeke IV至少有2个儿子。她去世于1385年4月在英国素瑞可洛頓享年79岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Grey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

variants.name: Gray
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: Greiwe
meanings: German: possibly a nickname from Middle High German greibe ‘sharp, tangy, dour’. Compare Grey .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan de Grey Baroness Willoughby was born in 1306, in Rotherfield Greys, Oxfordshire, England, her father, Sir John de Grey 1st Baron Grey of Rotherfield, was 29 and her mother, Margaret Oddingseles, was 29. She had at least 2 sons with Sir John Leeke IV. She died in April 1385, in Croydon, Surrey, England, at the age of 79.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan de Grey Baroness Willoughby est née en 1306, à Rotherfield Greys, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John de Grey 1st Baron Grey of Rotherfield, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Margaret Oddingseles, avait 29 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils avec Sir John Leeke IV. Elle est décédée en avril 1385, à Croydon, Surrey, Angleterre, à l’âge de 79 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan de Grey Baroness Willoughby 1306 in Rotherfield Greys, Oxfordshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John de Grey 1st Baron Grey of Rotherfield 29 und ihre Mutter Margaret Oddingseles 29. Sie hatte mit Sir John Leeke IV mindestens 2 Söhne. Sie starb im April 1385 im Alter von 79 Jahren in Croydon, Surrey, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Grey Baroness Willoughby è nata il nel 1306 nel Rotherfield Greys, Oxfordshire, England, sua padre, Sir John de Grey 1st Baron Grey of Rotherfield, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Margaret Oddingseles, aveva 29 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli con Sir John Leeke IV. Morì nel aprile 1385, nel Croydon, Surrey, England, all'età di 79 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan de Grey Baroness Willoughbyが1306年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアロスエルフィールド グレイズで生まれた時，父Sir John de Grey 1st Baron Grey of Rotherfieldは29歳，母Margaret Oddingselesは29歳であった。彼女はSir John Leeke IVとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1385年4月にイギリススリークロイドンで79歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan de Grey Baroness Willoughby(이)가 1306년 영국 스퍼드실르 로더필드 그리스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John de Grey 1st Baron Grey of Rotherfield(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Oddingseles(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 Sir John Leeke IV(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1385년 4월 영국 수리 크로이던에서 79세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan de Grey Baroness Willoughby nasceu em 1306, em Rotherfield Greys, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John de Grey 1st Baron Grey of Rotherfield, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Oddingseles, tinha 29 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Sir John Leeke IV. Ela faleceu em abril de 1385, em Croydon, Surrey, Inglaterra, com 79 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan de Grey Baroness Willoughby родилась в 1306 г. в местности ротирфиелд грис, оксфордшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John de Grey 1st Baron Grey of Rotherfield было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Oddingseles, 29. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына от Sir John Leeke IV. Она умерла апрель 1385 г. в местности кроидон, сурри, Англия в возрасте 79 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan de Grey Baroness Willoughby nació en 1306, en Rotherfield Greys, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John de Grey 1st Baron Grey of Rotherfield, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Margaret Oddingseles, tenía 29 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Sir John Leeke IV. Murió en abril de 1385, en Croydon, Surrey, Inglaterra, a la edad de 79 años.
